description: A line that marks the limits of an area; a dividing line. 'Phorui' names the limit that defines—the border that creates identity by marking what is inside from what is outside.
sound_symbolism: The word establishes limits. 'Pho' /ɸo/ begins with breath marking, presence defined. 'Ru' rolls along the border. Finally 'i' arrives at certainty, boundary clear.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo phorui' (the boundary). Related to 'shuna' (edge) but emphasizes the concept of limit rather than the physical line.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Healthy boundaries enable relationship—knowing where I end and you begin allows true meeting.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Boundaries are conventions—useful for navigation but ultimately empty of inherent existence.
